title: 4. Diagnosis for Cosmetic Acupuncture
text:
(1) General conditions
Diagnosis is the ﬁrst and most important step in cosmetic acupuncture. We set up a
treatment plan following meticulous diagnosis through observation, interrogation, and pulse
checking. You should check the channel ﬂow, Stomach channel, Large Intestine channel,
Gallbladder channel, and Triple Burner channel, which are the major channels on the face.
The most commonly connected organs are the Lung, Kidney, and Spleen. The Lung governs
the skin in ﬁve elements. The Kidney plays a major role in aging. The Spleen nourishes the
skin and muscles. Also, the Liver is important because stress and qi stagnation are deeply
related to it.
Depression and insomnia should also be cared for regardless of the skin itself. A distorted
body image or low self-esteem is important to be considered. When the patient has these
conditions, the treatment goal can be difﬁcult to meet, and their interpretation of the results
can be very different from the practitioner’s perspective.
(2) Cosmetic history
Check the patient’s history of cosmetic interventions like plastic surgery, Botox, and ﬁller.
They can affect cosmetic acupuncture directly and also affect the patient’s expectations and
create a result discrepancy. When a patient has had repetitive cosmetic surgeries and has a
distorted body image, it may be very difﬁcult to get treatment satisfaction. Sometimes, we
need  to  recommend  seeing  a  psychiatrist  or  therapist  ﬁrst.  For  the  best  results,  a
comprehensive assessment in the initial treatment stage is recommended.
(3) Wrinkles
Static wrinkles vs Dynamic wrinkles
Static wrinkles are wrinkles that exist regardless of the facial expression. They are always
there. On the other hand, dynamic wrinkles only appear or deepen with certain expressions.
Once we get older, dynamic wrinkles also become static wrinkles. Dynamic wrinkles are
related to repetitive muscle movements and facial posture, so they are more manageable and
respond better than static wrinkles to cosmetic acupuncture.
~ !  ~
56
Facial  Cosmetic Acupuncture
Vertical wrinkles vs Horizontal wrinkles
Horizontal wrinkles are horizontal lines such as the forehead lines. Forehead lines are
typical dynamic wrinkles. Even young children can make them. On the other hand, vertical
wrinkles are vertical lines, such as the frown lines. They show up with the progress of aging
and  are  made  with  repetitive  muscle  movements  over  pretty  long  time.  Consequently,
vertical lines are not easy to improve.
(4) Types of skin
Individual skin type is important not only for choosing the right skin care products but
also for cosmetic acupuncture.
Dry skin tends to be sensitive to stimulation, so choosing ﬁner needles is recommended,
and after the treatment, hydration with a mask pack or other skin care should follow.
On the other hand, oily skin is usually relatively strong and thick, so thicker needles are
okay. Before treatment and each needle insertion, wiping with a sterile alcohol pad makes
handling of the needle easier and keeps the needle from slipping due to the constant oil
secretion of the face.
~ !  ~
57
Facial  Cosmetic Acupuncture
5.
The 5Cs of Cosmetic Acupuncture
These are the essential steps of cosmetic acupuncture practice.
All of them start with letter C.
~ !  ~
58
1C - Consent
2C - Clean
3C -
Concentrate
4C - Count
5C - Care
Facial  Cosmetic Acupuncture
1C - Consent
Before starting treatment, the whole treatment procedure and possible side effects should
be explained. Conﬁrm that a patient fully understands. A consent form must be signed by the
patient before treatment.
2C - Clean
